posture: 5+ year stance: the household is ALREADY diversified across 8 asset classes (Core Equity 26.2%, Dividend 18.3%, Individual Stocks 18.2% with NVDA ~18% strategic, Cash 12.9%, Defensive 11.1%, Growth 10.9%, International 2.4%, Alternative 0%) and is on paper FI under a 3.5% SWR (11.2M NIS × 0.035 = 392k > 277k canonical spend). The work is NOT a transition from a single concentrated position — that framing was incorrect in synth #19 and is retracted. The work IS: (a) reduce NVDA from its RSU-driven lot peaks toward a 15% strategic cap by 2031, (b) migrate residual US-domiciled non-NVDA holdings to UCITS to drive the US-situs estate tail from $1.37M toward <$1M, (c) accumulate ~5 more years of salary+RSU+ESPP toward the 22M NIS / age-49 target while preserving capital-preservation drawdown discipline, (d) close the will/POA/guardianship gap. The plan's 3.5% SWR framing is retained as the headline 'FI achieved' lens while capital-preservation (2.4% real yield) is the binding decision rule, and the gap between the two is acknowledged rather than papered over.

targets.label: Gross long-horizon FI base (2031 target, returns-only basis)
targets.rationale: 22M NIS 2031 target from the user's plan baseline (Jacobs_Wealth_Plan). Reaffirmed against 277k canonical spend with a buffer band above the 11.54M NIS bare-FI threshold. Achievable with current ~11.2M base + ~360k NIS/yr bank-side savings + ~$315k/yr net RSU accumulation over 5 years.

targets.label: Education carve-out (combined household contribution, 2 kids)
targets.rationale: 500k NIS per child × 2 (Adva, Geva); codified in prior cycle and preserved.

targets.label: Net FI-eligible base (gross minus education carve-out)
targets.rationale: Gross 22M NIS minus 1M NIS education carve-out = 21M NIS net base against which capital-preservation FI is measured.

targets.label: Required real yield floor on net FI base
targets.rationale: 277,004 NIS canonical spend / 21,000,000 NIS net base = 1.32% real yield floor — well below the plan's 4.5% real-return assumption, leaving headroom for multiple-compression and FX drag.

targets.label: Bare-FI threshold at 2.4% real yield (returns-only, 277k spend)
targets.rationale: 277,004 / 0.024 ≈ 11.54M NIS — bare returns-only FI under capital-preservation. Current ~11.2M leaves only ~300k NIS margin (~2.7% buffer) vs the plan's 3.5% SWR framing that implies 3.3M cushion. Acknowledged tighter under user's elected drawdown style.

targets.label: Info-tech sector cap on MANAGED PORTFOLIO (excludes NVDA RSU income stream)
targets.rationale: 35% sector cap on managed portfolio; NVDA RSU income-stream lots excluded from denominator per architectural decision. Tech-sector glidepath question remains open with user.

targets.label: US-situs new-position risk-acceptance ceiling
targets.rationale: Risk-acceptance ceiling on NEW US-situs positions, not a convergence target on existing stack. Existing US-situs ~$3.09M is being migrated via UCITS-first deployment.

targets.label: US-situs estate tail (current exposure estimate)
targets.rationale: Accepted prior-round delta preserved. 40% × ($3.5M US-situs aggregate − $60k NRA exemption) ≈ $1.37M. PlanCritique snapshot-based recomputation today shows ~$1.21M; the $1.37M target retains a slight safety overhead pending Atlanta SFR / incidental items reconciliation.

targets.label: NVDA strategic share of portfolio (2031 target)
targets.rationale: From RSU-driven lot peaks (snapshot 11,471 sh ≈ 64.9% of $3,540k tradeable book, but ~18% strategic per user's 8-asset-class diversification reading) toward 15% strategic by 2031 via the 3,000 sh/yr cadence.

themes.label: Portfolio is already diversified — work is NVDA-cap reduction + UCITS migration + 5-year accumulation, NOT a de-risking transition
themes.rationale: Snapshot supports 8-asset-class diversification (Core Equity 26.2%, Dividend 18.3%, Individual Stocks 18.2%, Cash 12.9%, Defensive 11.1%, Growth 10.9%, International 2.4%, Alternative 0%) per user directive. NVDA strategic share is ~18%, not 61%; the 61% figure reflected an RSU lot peak. Prior synth #19's 'single concentrated stock to durable portfolio' framing was wrong and is retracted.

themes.label: Capital-preservation / returns-only drawdown is binding decision rule; 3.5% SWR remains documentary headline
themes.rationale: User has elected capital_preservation_returns_only; bare-FI threshold under that rule is 11.54M NIS, materially tighter than the plan's 3.5% SWR 'cleared by 3.3M' framing. Both views are tracked so the decision rule (capital preservation) governs while the headline (FI achieved on paper) remains intact.

themes.label: UCITS-first deployment is the binding architectural choice for new US-equity exposure
themes.rationale: Non-US-person status + US-situs estate exposure makes Ireland-domiciled UCITS (CSPX, ACWD, FWRA, CNDX) the only durable vehicle for new US-equity exposure. Confirmed GREEN by PlanCritique; dividend WHT economics roughly neutral under the US-Israel treaty.

themes.label: Estate-documents gap (will, POA, healthcare directive, guardianship) is a top-three structural risk
themes.rationale: will_exists=false, no POA, no healthcare directive, no guardianship designation for 2 minors aged 6 and 10 across IL/RO/US. Form 706-NA filing burden unavoidable on heirs while US-situs >$60k. Independent of UCITS migration pace.

themes.label: Education 1M NIS carve-out is netted; 1.5M NIS per-child apartment goal is kid-side aspirational, not a household liability
themes.rationale: Distinction preserved from prior cycle: education funding is a household obligation netted against the FI base; apartment goal is the kid's target, tracked but not deducted.

themes.label: Broad-market multiple-compression risk on the 4.5% real-return assumption
themes.rationale: MacroAnalyst regime=neutral (10Y 4.45%, WTI $97.63 stagflation drag); fleet-level multiples stretched outside NVDA (TSLA PEG 31 on -39% earnings growth, AMD PEG 3.07, AMZN EV/EBITDA 115). Accepted prior-round delta preserved; treat as parameterized stress on plan's 4.5% real-return baseline, not base case.

themes.label: Tech-sector cap glidepath — user counter-position remains unresolved
themes.rationale: Concern about info-tech sector concentration on the managed-portfolio denominator (excluding NVDA RSU stream) remains open; last user DISAGREE response addressed the wrong item.

actions.label: Continue NVDA single-name deconcentration toward 15% strategic by 2031
actions.detail: Maintain the 3,000 sh/yr cadence (user-confirmed; intake nvda_sale_progress.target_shares=3,000). At constant price, share-count glide: 11,471 → ~8,471 (end-2026) → ~5,471 (end-2027) → ~2,471 (end-2028) → 15% strategic by 2031.
actions.rationale: Aligns realized cadence with the user's stated 15% 2031 strategic cap and avoids surtax-band acceleration that the plan's legacy 10,000 sh/yr 2026 schedule would force.

actions.label: Migrate residual US-domiciled non-NVDA holdings to UCITS over 24 months
actions.detail: Phase-out of SCHD, SCHG, VOO, QQQM, SPMO, VTV, SGOV and similar US-domiciled wrappers in favor of CSPX / ACWD / FWRA / CNDX (Irish-domiciled, foreign-corp shares, not US-situs). Target: drive US-situs estate tail from $1.37M toward <$1M by end-2028.
actions.rationale: Materially reduces 706-NA filing burden on heirs and Form 706-NA 40%-above-$60k exposure; dividend economics roughly neutral under treaty.

actions.label: Execute estate-documentation program (will, guardianship, POA, healthcare directive, living trust)
actions.detail: Engage cross-border (IL/RO/US) attorney; draft will addressing each jurisdiction's intestate regime; designate guardianship for Adva and Geva; execute POA and healthcare directive; decide on living trust for minor beneficiaries.
actions.rationale: PlanCritique RED on estate-documents gap; will_exists=false with 2 minor children and tri-jurisdictional assets is asymmetric tail risk independent of UCITS migration pace.

actions.label: Codify gross/net FI split and capital-preservation framing in goals_yaml
actions.detail: Persist gross 22M NIS, education 1M NIS carve-out, net 21M NIS, 277k NIS spend, 2.4% real yield floor, and 11.54M NIS bare-FI threshold as structured fields in goals_yaml; flag plan's 3.5% SWR as documentary, capital-preservation as binding.
actions.rationale: Prevents recurrence of the 500k NIS spend / 10.3M NIS FI drift PlanCritique flagged in prior versions; gives downstream agents a canonical source of truth.

actions.label: Re-route tech-sector glidepath question to user in next cycle
actions.detail: Surface the unrefuted tech-sector concentration concern (managed-portfolio denominator, excluding NVDA RSU) explicitly and separately from the NVDA RSU question.
actions.rationale: Last DISAGREE addressed wrong item; concern remains open and requires user judgment.

actions.label: Re-validate 22M NIS gross midpoint at next annual PlanCritique cycle
actions.detail: Treat 22M NIS as a working midpoint; re-derive against 277k spend × buffer band each annual cycle.
actions.rationale: Annual freshness convention for long horizon.

deltas_from_prior.summary: Add explicit 'already-diversified across 8 asset classes' theme; retract prior synth #19's incorrect 'single concentrated stock to durable portfolio' framing.

proposed.label: Portfolio is already diversified — work is NVDA-cap reduction + UCITS migration + 5-year accumulation, NOT a de-risking transition

deltas_from_prior.rationale: User directive Wave 8 v2.3: snapshot shows 8-asset-class diversification with NVDA at ~18% strategic; the 61% figure reflects an RSU lot peak, not the strategic position. The transition framing is incorrect and must be retracted.

deltas_from_prior.summary: Re-add explicit NVDA strategic 15% target by 2031 (lineage to prior draft #18 target same id).

proposed.label: NVDA strategic share of portfolio (2031 target)

deltas_from_prior.rationale: User directive explicitly anchors the 15% strategic target by 2031 as the end-state of the 3,000 sh/yr cadence; reuses item_id from prior draft #18 since intent is identical.

rationale: Long horizon remains intentionally sticky on substrate (22M NIS gross / 21M net FI base, 1.32% real yield floor, 11.54M bare-FI threshold, $1.37M US-situs tail, UCITS-first architecture, NVDA 15% strategic by 2031, education carve-out, estate-documents gap as top-three risk). The minor revision this cycle is two-fold: (a) retract the incorrect 'single concentrated stock to durable portfolio' framing — the household is already diversified across 8 asset classes per the user directive and the snapshot, with NVDA at ~18% strategic, not 61% — and re-frame the long-horizon work as NVDA-cap reduction + UCITS migration + 5-year accumulation; (b) re-anchor the NVDA strategic 15% target explicitly into the long-horizon target stack (reusing prior draft #18's item_id since intent is identical). Capital-preservation remains the binding decision rule with 3.5% SWR retained as documentary headline. Confidence MEDIUM: macro/FX/multiples data are LIVE but the plan's 4.5% real-return assumption needs explicit multiple-compression-stress treatment, and tax-substrate domain files still carry last_verified=1900-01-01 pending domain-refresh.
